title: impl AsRef<[u8]> for label parameters — defensible against narrowing to &str or &[u8]
description: key_cache, nvm_add, nvm_overwrite, cert_add_trusted all accept label: impl AsRef<[u8]> rather than &str or &[u8]. The wolfHSM NVM label field is a 24-byte opaque byte array with no enforced encoding — callers may use ASCII strings, byte arrays, or structured binary labels. Accepting AsRef<[u8]> lets callers pass &str, &[u8], String, Vec<u8>, or fixed-size arrays without conversion. A contributor might suggest narrowing to &str (more ergonomic for the common case) or &[u8] (simpler). &str breaks callers with binary labels; &[u8] loses ergonomic From impls. The AsRef bound is the correct choice for an opaque-bytes field.
